data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add` only for legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges; never the parent.
3. Use `parent=<parent-id>` metadata and wire `parent -> child`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Don't mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; don't parallelize `git add`, `git commit`, or staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; subject ends with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add` for child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-e2fd88f3">
    <title>Classify land coordination failures without implementation reruns: part 1</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-bced4e39 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Niflheim land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures were recorded as overall `execution_failed` even when the candidate had succeeded or landed. The worker retried implementation work instead of reconciling land/evidence state or sending the bead to operator attention, wasting route attempts and confusing system failure with model-quality failure.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:305-309` wraps `LandingAdvancer` errors as generic `land advancer` errors.
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` contains the normative table that only `OutcomeGenuineFailure` can escalate, but execute-bead/work land outcomes are not consistently reduced to this typed table before retry/escalation decisions.

PROPOSED FIX
Add self-contained Niflheim-style land coordination fixtures or table-driven cases under `cli/internal/agent/...` for `cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate or merge is already landed, and for `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s` caused by generated DDx evidence. Classify these as land/ref/index/evidence coordination outcomes, not implementation/model failures. If the candidate commit or merge is already an ancestor of the target, reconcile and close/continue without rerunning implementation. If the landing worktree is dirty with generated evidence or DDx metadata, retry/preserve the land/evidence step or move to operator attention according to existing reliability policy.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`; reuse or align with it.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`; this is typed classification and reconciliation behavior, not another timeout implementation.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f`. Dependencies to preserve: `ddx-2965925e`, `ddx-b2846610`, `ddx-d67f124e`. Related: `ddx-e1a576a7`, `ddx-a9d130d0`, `ddx-ac10e7af`. Governing references: FEAT-010, TP-021, ADR-024, docs/dev/engineer
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- `TestExecuteBeadLoop_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationFailureDoesNotRerunImplementation` adds self-contained Niflheim evidence cases for both observed details: `cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate/merge is already landed, and `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s`; it asserts the selected action is reconcile, retry-land, preserve-evidence, or operator-attention, not a fresh implementation run.
- `TestWorkRetryEscalation_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationDoesNotRaiseMinPower` proves land/ref/index/evidence-staging outcomes do not raise requested min power/profile and do not request stronger model escalation.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. `TestExecuteBeadLoop_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationFailureDoesNotRerunImplementation` adds self-contained Niflheim evidence cases for both observed details: `cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate/merge is already landed, and `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s`; it asserts the selected action is reconcile, retry-land, preserve-evidence, or operator-attention, not a fresh implementation run.
2. `TestWorkRetryEscalation_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationDoesNotRaiseMinPower` proves land/ref/index/evidence-staging outcomes do not raise requested min power/profile and do not request stronger model escalation.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:iterate, area:agent, area:execute-bead, area:workers, area:review, kind:bug, kind:reliability, severity:critical, spec:FEAT-010, spec:TP-021, adr:024, incident:niflheim-2026-06-06, prevention, decomposed</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-bced4e39" base-rev="2b974000609ad30a1bb4b6451e75a8d0c9248aa1" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260628T010156-3561d994"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read them first. Ground decisions in repository state; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented the land-coordination power-floor regression coverage in [cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_coordination_power_test.go](/home/erik/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-e2fd88f3-20260628T010156-3561d994/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_coordination_power_test.go#L15).

- `TestWorkRetryEscalation_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationDoesNotRaiseMinPower` now covers both staged-evidence land coordination cases:
  - generated DDx evidence staged, selects `retry_land`
  - source work staged, selects `operator_attention`
- The test asserts:
  - no implementation rerun
  - no stronger-model escalation
  - requested/inferred power stays `standard`
  - durable execute-bead audit records the expected retry action
- The loop-side regression test already exists in [cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go](/home/erik/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-e2fd88f3-20260628T010156-3561d994/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go#L848) and was included in verification.
- Verified with:
  - `go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestExecuteBeadLoop_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationFailureDoesNotRerunImplementation|TestWorkRetryEscalation_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationDoesNotRaiseMinPower|TestWorkRetryEscalation_NiflheimEvidence_DecomposedParentDoesNotRaiseMinPower'`
  - `go test ./internal/agent/executeloop -run 'TestTryLoopStateMachine.*'`
- `lefthook run pre-commit` still fails on pre-existing unrelated `internal/agent` tests:
  - `TestPreDispatchDirtyPreserveRequiresStableImplementationDirt`
  - `TestAttemptProviderChildrenAreRouteScoped`
- Commit: `f478215aa` with message `test(agent): add land coordination min-power regression [ddx-e2fd88f3]`
